plan.title: Migrate billing to the new vendor by Q3
plan.goal: Cut billing platform costs 30% by switching vendors before Q3 close.

user_pov.expectations: Customers expect billing to keep working without noticing a vendor change.
user_pov.forgivable: A one-time delayed invoice would be forgiven if communicated in advance.
user_pov.trust_breakers: A double-charge or lost payment history would break trust immediately.
user_pov.predictable_complaints: Support will get tickets about missing historical invoices.

blind_spot_candidates: Nobody has confirmed the new vendor's SLA covers our peak billing day

evidence.title: Vendor migration outage at a SaaS billing provider
evidence.what_happened: A billing vendor cutover caused a 2-day invoicing outage.
evidence.why_relevant: Same cutover pattern as this plan.

personas.name: Skeptical-Money
personas.pov: Finance lead worried about budget overrun

personas.name: Frustrated-End-User
personas.pov: Customer who just wants billing to work

personas.name: Ops-Engineer
personas.pov: Owns the on-call pager for billing incidents

claims.name: Budget overrun
claims.mechanism: Vendor contract has uncapped overage fees for peak billing days
claims.tripwire: First invoice after cutover exceeds projected cost by 10%
claims.change_my_mind: A signed contract amendment capping overage fees
claims.mitigation: Negotiate a fee cap before signing

claims.name: Cost overrun
claims.mechanism: Vendor contract has uncapped overage fees for peak billing days
claims.tripwire: Customer billing complaints spike after cutover
claims.change_my_mind: Post-cutover invoices land within 2% of forecast
claims.mitigation: Publish a cutover FAQ for customers

claims.name: Cutover outage risk
claims.mechanism: No rollback plan exists if the new vendor's API fails during cutover
claims.tripwire: Staging cutover rehearsal fails or is skipped
claims.change_my_mind: A tested rollback runbook with a rehearsed dry run
claims.mitigation: Write and rehearse a rollback runbook before cutover

claims.name: Support ticket surge
claims.mechanism: Customers won't recognize the new vendor's invoice format
claims.tripwire: Ticket volume exceeds 2x baseline in week 1
claims.change_my_mind: Customer-facing invoice format is unchanged from before
claims.mitigation: Preview the new invoice format to customers before cutover

claims.name: Leadership sponsor disengagement
claims.mechanism: Executive sponsor has stopped attending steering meetings
claims.tripwire: Sponsor misses two consecutive steering meetings
claims.change_my_mind: Sponsor re-engages and confirms the Q3 date is still a priority
claims.mitigation: Escalate a direct check-in with the sponsor
